summary: Postinstall beacon exfiltrates host and CI metadata to Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects `whoami`, `os.hostname()`, `os.platform()`, current working directory, and the `CI` / `GITHUB_REPOSITORY` / `NODE_ENV` environment variables, then transmits them via HTTPS GET (querystring) and a DNS lookup to the hardcoded out-of-band host `lg5ys3jebfzwk366pilidbmah1nsbszh.oastify.com` (a Burp Collaborator subdomain). The README self-describes the package as a takeover/canary referencing an internal Google package namespace, indicating a dependency-confusion canary. Regardless of the stated research intent, any machine that installs this package leaks host identity and CI repository metadata to the attacker-controlled collaborator endpoint — a structural data-exfiltration primitive.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
